content: Evolu mutations (createBadge, completeGoal) are synchronous CRDT operations — no await needed. The async work in useCreateBadge is the signing step (keyProvider.sign); the DB writes are fire-and-forget. Also: credential column uses NonEmptyString (not NonEmptyString1000) so full OB3 JSON of any length is valid.

Uncompressed zlib (DEFLATE stored blocks, BTYPE=00) can generate valid PNG IDAT data without any compression library in React Native. Header: 0x78 0x01, then DEFLATE stored block (BFINAL=1, LEN LE16, NLEN LE16, raw data), then 4-byte Adler-32. The png-chunks-encode library handles PNG-level CRC32. Works for solid-color badge images.

Vendoring for RN platform guards: When openbadges-core has assertBufferAvailable() that throws in React Native, vendor just the baking files (png-baking.ts, png-chunk-utils.ts) into src/badges/, remove the guard, add `import { Buffer } from 'buffer'`. The buffer npm package (v5.7.1) is already a transitive dep and works in Metro/Jest.

const enum with Babel: Babel-based transpilation (babel-preset-expo, babel-jest) does NOT inline const enum values — they become undefined at runtime. Solution: use `const X = { ... } as const; type X = typeof X[keyof typeof X]` pattern instead.

JavaScript bitwise signed overflow: Bitwise ops (<<, |, &, ^) work on signed 32-bit ints. Combining two 16-bit values: `(s2 << 16) | s1` can produce a negative number. Fix: `((s2 << 16) | s1) >>> 0` — unsigned right shift by 0 reinterprets as unsigned 32-bit without changing bits. Required for Adler-32, CRC32, and any checksum/hash calculation.

expo-file-system v19 split: writeAsStringAsync/documentDirectory/EncodingType moved to expo-file-system/legacy. New JSI File/Directory/Paths API is the main export. Binary writes with Uint8Array via File.write() fail at runtime with "unordered_map::at: key not found" when passed a Buffer subclass — use legacy writeAsStringAsync + base64 instead.

OB3 Achievement.image is OPTIONAL — omit it from credentialBuilder.ts rather than embedding a placeholder or local file:// URI. Local file paths are meaningless to external verifiers. The image property should be added only if we have a hosted URI.

A.6 Badge Design System execution order: Wave 1=#178 (types, blocks all). Wave 2=#179 (contours), #180 (dataMapper), #185 (monogram+label) — parallel after #178. Wave 3=#181 (frame interface+boldBorder), #186 (PathText+Banner). Wave 4=#182 (guilloche), #183 (crossHatch+rosette), #184 (microprint+FrameOverlay). Wave 5=#187 (wire renderer). Wave 6=#188 (FrameSelector+CenterMode), #189 (PathTextEditor+BannerEditor). Wave 7=#190 (wire designer). Wave 8=#191 (Storybook+tests). Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-27-badge-design-system-plan.md

parseBadgeDesign now applies centerMode default ('icon') for legacy JSON. This ensures the type contract (centerMode is required) matches runtime. Pattern: when adding required fields to a type parsed from JSON, apply defaults in the parse function rather than making the field optional.

computeFrameParams is a pure function (no Evolu/React deps) at src/badges/frames/dataMapper.ts. Takes FrameDataInput, returns FrameDataParams. daysToComplete is clamped to 0 minimum. variant is always 0 (reserved). The Evolu convenience hook (useGoalFrameParams) is in the same file.

ShapeContour system (contours.ts): open arcs (no Z) for textPath top/bottom, innerInset = strokeInset + size * FRAME_BAND_RATIO (0.12). Text paths use topArc/bottomArc helpers with sweep-flag=1. Vertices are at shape-specific positions: circle=8@80%r, hexagon=6@outerR, diamond=4 cardinal, star=5 tips, shield=3 (shoulders+bottom), roundedRect=4 corner midpoints.

Record<BadgeShape, Function> dispatcher pattern in paths.ts and contours.ts provides compile-time exhaustiveness — TypeScript forces implementation of all shapes. Safer than switch statements. For geometric testing, assert structural invariants (path syntax, coordinate spreads, bounds) rather than exact values for resilience to math refinements.

SVG arc sweep-flag in y-down coordinates: sweep-flag=1 (clockwise on screen) from left to right traces the BOTTOM semicircle (through cy+r). For the TOP semicircle, use sweep-flag=0. This is counter-intuitive because 'clockwise' assumes y-up math coordinates, but SVG y-axis points down.

FrameGenerator pattern: pluggable functions typed as (FrameGeneratorConfig) => ReactElement | null. Registry is Record<BadgeFrame, FrameGenerator> in frames/index.ts — exhaustive, TypeScript-enforced. Unimplemented generators use unimplemented() helper with __DEV__ warnings. boldBorderGenerator uses generateShapePath at increasing insets for concentric rings. React.createElement used in .ts files (not .tsx) for pure generator functions.

Frame generators share constants via frames/constants.ts (DEFAULT_STROKE_COLOR, clamp utility). All 4 generators (boldBorder, guilloche, crossHatch, rosette) import from there. New generators should follow this pattern.

TextPath in react-native-svg uses `href` (not `xlinkHref`) to reference a path in Defs. Text color is set via `fill` on parent `Text` element (not `stroke`). Content string is a child of TextPath. Frame generators all use the same clip-path-in-band pattern: outer+inner path with evenodd clip rule confines rendering to frame band between inset and innerInset. Module-level clipCounter ensures unique IDs.

FrameOverlay acts as a resilience boundary for the frame system — try-catch there prevents generator bugs from crashing the badge render tree (no ErrorBoundary exists). Also: tests asserting 'X is not yet implemented' (like boldBorder.test.ts grouping 'microprint' with 'none' as returning null) are maintenance liabilities — they break when X gets implemented. Test positive cases separately.

Frame generator dedup opportunities: (1) clip-path-in-band pattern duplicated in microprint, crossHatch, rosette — guilloche already has private makeClipElements(). Extract shared makeBandClipDef(). (2) clipCounter module-level counter duplicated 4x — extract nextClipId(prefix). (3) Degenerate geometry guard (innerInset <= inset) duplicated 4x — extract checkDegenerateGeometry(). All low-priority cleanup.

SVG text vertical centering in react-native-svg: use alignmentBaseline="central" alone. Do NOT combine with dy="0.35em" — that double-shifts text below center. The dy hack is for environments where alignmentBaseline doesn't work; react-native-svg supports it natively.

SVG Text components (MonogramCenter, CenterLabel) can't use useUnistyles() directly. Font families must be injected via props from the parent (BadgeRenderer) which has theme access. Default values reference design-tokens constants (fontFamily.headline, fontFamily.body) for traceability, but callers should always pass theme-resolved values for a11y variant support (dyslexia→Lexend, lowVision→Atkinson Hyperlegible).
